A [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]] owned and operated by an unknown independent party, '''''Mercury''''' at one point aided the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] by exiting hyperspace near to where the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Razor (CR90 corvette)|''Razor'']] was captured by [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']]. She picked up [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] [[V33-1XP]] carrying a lone Rebel crewman, who had with him the [[Death Star II plans|plans]] for the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]].

''Mercury'' then took the escape pod to the rendezvous point with the [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] and launched the escape pod again to reach the Cruiser. ''Avenger'' though showed up again along with the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. The crew of the ''Independence'', however, managed to protect the pod while it was moving to the Cruiser. The Mercury fled the scene as it did not possess the firepower to fend off either the ''Avenger'' or the ''Executor''.

==Behind the scenes==
''Mercury'' plays the role of a "deus ex machina" since she inexplicably shows up to help the Rebels in the darkest hour without any introductions. The nature and affiliation of the ''Mercury'', as well as the reason she arrived to the Rebels' aid, is not taken in consideration in the game scenario.

{{Ship-stub}}
